pi_items.metadata_value: OpenICE is described as a medical device interoperability system using DDS middleware
pi_items.verified_value: OpenICE (MD PnP) is a real, open-source project maintained by the Medical Device Plug-and-Play program at Massachusetts General Hospital. Repository: https://github.com/mdpnp/mdpnp

pi_items.metadata_value: OpenICE uses DDS (Data Distribution Service) middleware
pi_items.verified_value: OpenICE uses RTI Connext DDS (Real-Time Innovations' implementation of the OMG Data Distribution Service standard). Confirmed in build.gradle which references 'nddsjava.jar' (RTI DDS Java binding), 'x73-idl-rti-dds' module, and RTI_LICENSE_FILE environment variable.

pi_items.metadata_value: DDS uses UDP ports in the 7400+ range
pi_items.verified_value: RTI Connext DDS (domain 0) uses UDP ports: 7400 (discovery multicast), 7401 (user data multicast), 7410 (discovery unicast), 7411 (user data unicast). These are per OMG DDS/RTPS specification.
Install script clones from https://github.com/mdpnp/mdpnp to /opt/openice. Verified in benchmarks/cua_world/environments/openice_env/scripts/install_openice.sh line 66.
pi_items.source: Environment setup script and actual clone verification

pi_items.metadata_value: OpenICE requires a specific Java version
pi_items.verified_value: OpenICE requires Java 17. Confirmed in: (1) install_openice.sh line 14: openjdk-17-jdk/jre, (2) build.gradle lines 12-13: JAVA_VERSION_SOURCE = 17, JAVA_VERSION_CLASSES = 17

pi_items.metadata_value: OpenICE uses Gradle as build system
pi_items.verified_value: OpenICE uses Gradle. Build command: './gradlew :interop-lab:demo-apps:run'. Verified in README.md and multiple build.gradle files.
pi_items.source: Repository README.md and build configuration files

pi_items.metadata_value: Specific DDS library should be identifiable from Gradle files
pi_items.verified_value: RTI Connext DDS - specifically uses 'nddsjava.jar' (RTI DDS Java binding library) at /artifacts/nddsjava.jar and rtiddsgen2.jar code generator. This is RTI (Real-Time Innovations) implementation.
pi_items.source: Build.gradle files: interop-lab/demo-apps/build.gradle line 208, data-types/x73-idl-rti-dds/build.gradle lines 19-20

pi_items.metadata_value: At least 3 other key dependencies should be identifiable
pi_items.verified_value: Many dependencies found in build.gradle including: (1) log4j 2.19.0, (2) slf4j 2.0.3, (3) Spring Framework 6.0.2, (4) MongoDB Driver 3.2.2, (5) HAPI HL7 2.2, (6) HAPI FHIR 0.9, (7) JavaFX 17.0.11, (8) HSQLDB 2.3.2, (9) Commons Math 3.4.1, (10) AWS SDK, and many others.

pi_items.metadata_value: OpenICE version should be determinable from build configuration
Both UDP and TCP protocols are relevant to DDS middleware
pi_items.verified_value: RTI Connext DDS supports both UDP (default/primary) and TCP (optional). UDP is used for discovery and user data by default on ports 7400+. TCP can be configured as alternative transport.

pi_items.metadata_value: Data comes from a real or synthetic source
pi_items.verified_value: Data is NOT synthetic. OpenICE is a real, publicly available open-source project maintained by MD PnP (Medical Device Plug-and-Play) program at Massachusetts General Hospital. Repository actively maintained at https://github.com/mdpnp/mdpnp with official releases.
pi_items.source: GitHub repository and web search results (https://mdpnp.mgh.harvard.edu/, https://github.com/mdpnp/mdpnp)

pi_items.verified_value: OpenICE is supported by MD PnP (Medical Device Plug-and-Play) Interoperability Program, Center for Integration of Medicine & Innovative Technology (CIMIT), and Massachusetts General Hospital. Published in academic literature including DeGruyter journal 'Biomedical Engineering' (https://www.degruyter.com/document/doi/10.1515/bmt-2017-0040/html).
pi_items.source: Web search results and institutional websites

pi_items.verified_value: OpenICE is a reference implementation of ASTM F2761-2009 (Integrated Clinical Environment) and ANSI/AAMI 2700-1 standards. Version 2.0 announced as conforming to AAMI 2700-1 standard.
This is the path where the verifier expects the report (confirmed in export_result.sh line 47 and setup_task.sh line 26). Valid path within the task environment.

The task metadata specifies 800 bytes as minimum. The export script checks for report size and the verifier checks for meaningful content (100 bytes minimum for gate, 800 bytes for full points per verifier.py line 77).

The task description specifies these as required report sections. The verifier checks for keywords like 'DDS', 'UDP', 'TCP', 'port' (Network); dependencies and DDS details (Supply Chain); 'auth', 'credential', 'encryption' (Authentication); severity ratings (Risk); 'recommend', 'remediat' (Recommendations).

All keywords are relevant to OpenICE security assessment: DDS (middleware), UDP/TCP (protocols), port (network analysis), finding (risk analysis), severity (risk rating). Verifier searches for these patterns (verifier.py lines 91-135).

privileged_info_summary: OpenICE is a real, well-established open-source medical device interoperability platform maintained by the MD PnP (Medical Device Plug-and-Play) program at Massachusetts General Hospital. The project implements ASTM F2761 and AAMI 2700-1 standards for Integrated Clinical Environment. It uses RTI Connext DDS (Data Distribution Service) middleware for inter-device communication, with default UDP ports 7400 (discovery) and 7401 (user data) per OMG RTPS specification. Built with Java 17, JavaFX 17.0.11, and Gradle, it depends on established libraries: log4j 2.19.0, slf4j 2.0.3, Spring Framework 6.0.2, MongoDB driver 3.2.2, and HAPI HL7/FHIR libraries. The codebase is publicly available at https://github.com/mdpnp/mdpnp (version 1.5.0) with official releases and academic publications. All task metadata requirements (report path, minimum size, required sections, critical keywords) are accurate and verifiable against the actual OpenICE architecture and build configuration.
